_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning why it's important to keep trying when things are hard or new, and how effort helps us grow.

_source.w: We are learning why it's important to keep trying when things are hard or new, and how effort helps us grow.
y: Keeping going when things are tough helps our brains learn and get stronger.
Trying new things can feel scary, but it's how we discover what we're capable of.
Learning to push through challenges now builds the courage we need for bigger challenges later.
eg: We might try a tricky maths problem more than once and notice that it gets easier each time.
We could share a story about a time we couldn't do something at first but kept practising until we could.
We might cheer on a classmate who is finding something difficult, saying 'You can do it, keep going!'
We could talk about how learning to ride a bike or swim took lots of practice before it felt easy.
We might try a new food or game we've never tried before and talk about how it felt to give it a go.
We could draw a 'before and after' picture showing something we couldn't do at the start of the year but can do now.
